groups.title: A museum in Japan
groups.desc:
Questions 31-40
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.collect: • The museum accommodates a diverse range of artworks. • The museum is surrounded by $31$. • The design was based on a Chinese $32$ the architect knew. • Visitors can approach the museum through a $33$ and over a bridge. • After getting into the building, a glass $34$ can be noticed immediately. • Visitors feel like being $35$ because of the light. Advantages of underground houses: • There is less $36$. • They are at a lower risk of $37$. • They do better in energy conservation. Disadvantages of underground houses: • People may feel they do not have enough $38$ (but they are already used to $39$ shopping centres). • Places suffering from $40$ cannot be used to construct underground buildings.

questions.title: The design was based on a Chinese 【   】 the architect knew.
Visitors can approach the museum through a 【   】 and over a bridge.
After getting into the building, a glass 【   】 can be noticed immediately.
Visitors feel like being 【   】 because of the light.
They are at a lower risk of 【   】.
People may feel they do not have enough 【   】.
they are already used to 【   】 shopping centres
Places suffering from 【   】 cannot be used to construct underground buildings.

transcript.en: For the last few sessions, we've been looking at the different architecture for both their utilitarian purposes and aesthetic features as well.
In today's lecture, I want to continue this series by first introducing to you a unique underground museum in Japan.
And then, we'll discuss the pros and cons of underground architecture.
Now this museum was built in 1996, and around eighty percent of the museum is buried underground to preserve the lush natural beauty.
It houses an impressive collection of artworks.
The exhibits consist of works from ancient civilizations, including the Egyptians, Romans, and various Asian cultures, with the pieces mainly coming from the founder's private collection.
But perhaps the most intriguing aspect of the museum is the design of its structures and how they are integrated into their natural surroundings.
The museum is humbly embedded in mountains, close to a subway station connecting the nearest metropolis in about an hour.
The design was inspired by an ancient Chinese story titled 'Peach Blossom Valley'.
The architect read it when he was a schoolboy.
It is about a fisherman who loses his way, and his boat carries him upstream.
He is drawn into a cave by the fragrant scent of a forest of blossoming peach trees.
After travelling through a narrow road behind the cave, the man finds an idyllic village amid a splendid view.
The museum has been conceived as a real-world version of this village.
Visitors must pass through a tunnel first and then a cable bridge suspended over a gorge before arriving at the museum.
The museum's details reflect the designer's innovative endeavor to break new ground.
The architect changed the design numerous times to ensure the building was in harmony with the natural landscape.
It becomes evident as tourists walk around the building as the detail is very fastidious and makes for a unique experience.
Once visitors enter the museum, a sloping roof made of wide expanses of glass draws their attention right away.
The web-like structure filtered the light through and scattered it across the surface, making visitors feel like being outside with a vibrant backdrop of seasonal colours.
It is now clear that underground buildings have many advantages over traditional above-ground houses, and many architects are looking into the possibilities of this type of subsurface structure.
The most important advantage of these houses, especially in the current scenario of environmental degradation, is that they can better preserve indigenous flora and fauna.
But the advantages of underground architecture go beyond that.
For one thing, the earth surrounding the homes absorbs noise and thus provides natural soundproofing, creating a quiet living space with privacy.
Choosing an underground house will be peaceful and calming, even in a noisy region.
Moreover, there is less part of underground houses that can catch fire.
For the most part, wood is no longer needed for building a home underground.
The concrete foundations and metal structures of these homes all ensure good fireproofing.
Now underground houses also have the potential to save energy in the long run as they can tap the earth's heating or cooling power.
The earth's temperature doesn't vary as much as the temperature of the air outside.
It ensures that even though the climate outside fluctuates between extremes, the conditions inside the subterranean homes remain more or less stable.
However, there are also concerns over earth-sheltered homes.
There may be a psychological adjustment needed for a person shifting from a traditional above-ground house to an underground house.
Many people feel there is insufficient space in underground buildings.
But in fact, these people also admit that they often visited shopping centres located in the basement and did not feel claustrophobic.
In addition, underground houses cannot be built in regions experiencing flooding due to their unique geographical location.
And even for areas that are suited for underground structures, drainage should be planned carefully to prevent moisture retention.
While the advantages far outnumber their drawbacks, it may be a good idea to wait a little longer for these houses to come mainstream ...

31.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 artworks；提示词为 surrounded by，此处应填名词。
题目解析：本题上一句的定位词 artworks 出现较早，其后都是关于博物馆展品的描述，均不符合题干要求。继续往下听，听到 surroundings、embedded in，对应题干的 surrounded by，随后听到 mountains 一词，可判断其符合题干要求，填写下来即可。注意，后面出现的 subway station 既不符合题干要求，也不符合字数要求，可以排除。

32.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 Chinese；提示词为 a，此处应填单数可数名词。
题目解析：定位词 Chinese 原词重现，随即便听到符合题干要求的 story，先填写下来。之后听到题干的 architect knew 被替换为 architect read，可进一步确认答案。

33.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 visitors、bridge；提示词为 through a，此处应填靠近博物馆的设施类名词。
题目解析：定位词 visitors 原词重现，随即便听到符合题干要求的 tunnel，先填写下来。之后听到另一个定位词 bridge 原词重现，可进一步确认答案。

34.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 glass；提示词为 a，此处应填单数可数名词。
题目解析：先听到题干的 getting into the building 被替换为 enter the museum，随后听到的 roof 一词符合题干要求，并且能够和 glass 搭配使用，先填写下来。之后听到 glass 原词重现，可进一步确认答案。

35.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 light；提示词为 feel like being，此处应填副词。
题目解析：定位词 light 原词重现，随后听到与题干几乎一样的表达：visitors feel like being outside，将 outside 填写下来即可。

36.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 advantages、less，此处应填不可数名词。
题目解析：先听到逻辑信号词 for one thing，随后听到 absorbs，对应题干的 less，其后的 noise 一词符合题干要求，填写下来即可。注意，后面出现的 soundproofing 一词，与 less 搭配意为“隔音效果不好”，不符合题干的优点要求，可以排除。

37.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 lower risk，此处应填能与低风险搭配的名词。
题目解析：先听到逻辑信号词 moreover，随后听到能与 lower risk 搭配使用的 fire 一词，填写下来即可。考生若能注意到 lower risk 在原文被替换为 less part of underground houses ... catch fire 则更佳，但这并非做题的必要条件。注意，后面出现的 good fireproofing，不能与 lower risk 搭配使用，可以排除。

38.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 disadvantages、not enough，此处应填能与 not enough 搭配的名词。
题目解析：上一题结束后，先听到转折词 however，随后听到 concerns 一词，对应小标题的 disadvantages，可以定位到该题。之后听到 psychological adjustment，虽然能和 not enough 搭配使用，但不符合字数要求，可以排除。继续往下听，听到题干的 not enough 被替换为 insufficient，可判断与其搭配的 space 为正确答案。

39.analysis:
听前预测：定位词、提示词均为 shopping centres，此处应填修饰购物中心的名词或形容词。
题目解析：定位词 shopping centres 原词重现，随后听到能和 shopping centres 搭配使用的 basement 一词，填写下来即可。

40.analysis:
听前预测：提示词为 suffering from，此处应填能与 suffering from 搭配的名词。
题目解析：先听到逻辑信号词 in addition，随后听到题干的 cannot be used to construct 被替换为 cannot be built in，suffering from 被替换为 experiencing，可判断其后的 flooding 一词符合题干要求，填写下来即可。
